questions.question: What is a constitution?
questions.answer: A constitution is a set of basic rules that guide the government and citizens of a country.

questions.question: Which country followed the apartheid system?
questions.answer: South Africa followed the apartheid system.

questions.question: What was apartheid?
questions.answer: Apartheid was a system of racial discrimination in South Africa.

questions.question: Who led the struggle against apartheid?
questions.answer: Nelson Mandela led the struggle against apartheid.

questions.question: Which organization led the struggle against apartheid?
questions.answer: The African National Congress led the struggle against apartheid.

questions.question: What is the supreme law of a country?
questions.answer: The constitution is the supreme law of a country.

questions.question: What does a constitution determine?
questions.answer: A constitution determines the powers of the government and rights of citizens.

questions.question: What is the Constituent Assembly?
questions.answer: The Constituent Assembly is a body of representatives that drafted the Constitution of India.

questions.question: In which year was the Constituent Assembly formed?
questions.answer: The Constituent Assembly was formed in 1946.

questions.question: When was the Constitution of India adopted?
questions.answer: The Constitution of India was adopted on 26 November 1949.

questions.question: When did the Constitution of India come into effect?
questions.answer: It came into effect on 26 January 1950.

questions.question: Who was the chairman of the Drafting Committee?
questions.answer: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the chairman of the Drafting Committee.

questions.question: Who was the President of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.answer: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the President of the Constituent Assembly.

questions.question: What is the Preamble?
questions.answer: The Preamble is the introductory statement of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which words begin the Preamble of India?
questions.answer: The Preamble begins with 'We, the People of India'.

questions.question: What does 'Sovereign' mean?
questions.answer: Sovereign means a country has the power to make its own decisions.

questions.question: What does 'Secular' mean?
questions.answer: Secular means the state has no official religion and treats all religions equally.

questions.question: What does 'Republic' mean?
questions.answer: Republic means the head of the state is elected and not hereditary.

questions.question: What does 'Justice' mean in the Constitution?
questions.answer: Justice means fairness and equality for all citizens.

questions.question: What does 'Liberty' mean?
questions.answer: Liberty means freedom of thought, expression and belief.

questions.question: What does 'Equality' mean?
questions.answer: Equality means all citizens are equal before the law.

questions.question: What does 'Fraternity' mean?
questions.answer: Fraternity means a sense of brotherhood among citizens.

questions.question: Which day is celebrated as Republic Day in India?
questions.answer: 26 January is celebrated as Republic Day in India.

questions.question: How many members were in the Constituent Assembly of India?
questions.answer: The Constituent Assembly had 299 members.

questions.question: What does a constitution express about a country?
questions.answer: A constitution expresses the values and aspirations of the people.
